headline: Indian Community Bottom 40 Poverty Rate Unchanged at 11.4% for a Decade
context: Department of Statistics data shows the poverty rate among B40 Indian Malaysians has remained at approximately 11.4% since 2014, despite national B40 poverty declining from 8.2% to 5.3%.

cards.big: National B40 poverty fell from 8.2% to 5.3% in a decade.
cards.sub: Indian B40 poverty stayed at 11.4%. The rising tide lifted most boats but not this one.

cards.big: 3 dedicated Indian community development programmes were launched since 2014.
cards.sub: Combined budget: RM850M. Poverty rate: unchanged. The programmes are not reaching the poor.

cards.big: 65% of Indian poor live in former estate communities.
cards.sub: Estate restructuring in the 1990s displaced 300,000 people without adequate resettlement.

cards.big: Indian Malaysians hold 1.6% of corporate equity despite being 6.3% of the population.
cards.sub: No affirmative action programme targets Indian economic equity.

cards.big: RM850M spent, 0% poverty reduction. The money is going somewhere. It is not going to the Indian poor.
Indian B40 poverty is a policy failure — not a community failure. Targeted intervention must address estate displacement, land access, and educational underfunding simultaneously.
